name: Deriv Volatility 50 (1s variant exists as 1HZ50V)
trading_hours: 24/7/365 - never closes, no sessions, no news, no gaps

engine.how_it_moves: RNG-generated price at a constant 50% annualized volatility - the calmest of your three synthetics. Tick every ~2 seconds. Time of day is irrelevant.
engine.critical_implication: Smoothest synthetic = the best pure SCALPING vehicle on your entire list. Smaller wicks, fewer violent spikes, cleaner respect of intraday levels.

personality.summary: Steady and orderly. Trends are slower and smoother than V75/V100, ranges are cleaner and last longer, and stop-hunting wicks are the mildest of the three. Ideal for high-frequency scalping and for practicing execution. The trade-off: moves are smaller, so targets must be realistic and spread-aware.
behavioral_patterns: Ranges form frequently and respect their edges multiple times - range-fading works here better than on V75/V100
Trend legs are gradual staircases rather than explosions
M1 structure is actually usable on V50, unlike V100

key_technicals.moving_averages: M5 9/21 EMA ribbon defines the scalping trend; H1 50 EMA for overall bias
key_technicals.bollinger: BB(20,2) on M5 works well in ranges - band fades back to midline are consistent
key_technicals.structure: M5 BOS/CHoCH is clean and reliable; ranges give multiple honest touches before breaking
key_technicals.atr: Stops = 1.5-2x ATR(M5) - tighter than V75/V100 is acceptable here
key_technicals.stochastic: Stoch(5,3,3) on M1 times entries at M5 zones effectively on this index

scalping_plan.sessions: Any hour 24/7 - your primary high-frequency instrument

steps: Confirm range: H1 flat, M5 price oscillating between two zones with 2+ touches each
At an edge touch, wait for M1 rejection candle + Stoch(5,3,3) cross from extreme
Enter toward mid-range, SL 1.5x ATR(M5) beyond the edge
TP1 at BB midline / range middle, TP2 at opposite edge
M5 9 EMA above 21 EMA (or below for shorts) with clean structure = scalping trend
Enter on M1 close back in trend direction after a tag of the 21 EMA
SL beyond the pullback swing, TP 1:1 to 1:1.5, repeat on each pullback

scalping_plan.risk: 1% max per scalp. V50's danger is overtrading, not volatility: set a max of 10-15 trades per session and stop at your daily loss cap regardless of how 'safe' it feels.

steps: Track the mature M15 range; when one edge finally breaks with an M15 momentum close, trade the retest
SL inside the range midpoint, target = measured move of the range height
Trend days are slower here - trail on M15 and be patient

after_hours_00_05_sast.verdict: FULLY TRADEABLE - your safest instrument for the 00:00-05:00 SAST window. Recommended default night instrument.
playbook: Run setup_1 and setup_2 exactly as in daytime
Ideal pairing for the window: V50 for volume of trades, V75 for the one or two trend trades
Fatigue rule still applies: predefine trade count and loss cap before the session

invalidations_and_traps: Small moves tempt oversized positions - size by ATR-based stop, never by 'it barely moves'
Spread eats a larger fraction of each V50 scalp - minimum TP must be at least 3x spread
When V50 finally breaks a mature range, do NOT keep fading the edges - regime has changed
Boredom trades kill V50 accounts: no valid zone = no trade, even at 3am
